keyword.trend_momentum: accelerating — NIST AI Agent Standards Initiative Q1 2026, OWASP State of Agentic AI Security & Governance 1.0 (March 2026), EU AI Act enforcement phase, FTC enforcement actions

selection_rationale.why_this_keyword: Agentic AI Governance is the natural next topic for AgentForge's editorial pipeline. The first run covered orchestration (architecture); governance is the operational and compliance layer enterprises need immediately after adopting orchestration. It bridges the 'how to build' (May 23) with 'how to run safely' (today).
timeliness_factors: NIST launched AI Agent Standards Initiative and issued RFI (due March 9, 2026) — creating urgency for enterprises
OWASP published 'State of Agentic AI Security and Governance 1.0' (March 2026) — first comprehensive framework
FTC imposed 20-year audit order on AI vendor for false claims — enforcement is real
EU AI Act enforcement phase begins for high-risk AI systems
Texas HB 149, South Korea AI Basic Law, China algorithmic transparency — global regulatory wave
Forbes: 'Agentic AI Is Changing The Security Model For Enterprise Systems' (March 7, 2026)
selection_rationale.domain_alignment: Strong — AgentForge's core domain is AI agents and enterprise automation. Governance is the #1 concern CTOs raise after seeing orchestration demos. This article positions AgentForge as a trusted advisor on the full agent lifecycle.
selection_rationale.differentiation_from_prior_run: The May 23 article covered AI agent orchestration (build). This article covers governance (run safely). Together they form a complete enterprise agent lifecycle narrative. No overlap — complementary.

article_plan.tone: Enterprise B2B, authoritative but accessible — learnings from May 23 audit: target Flesch 35+, include 3+ plain-language interludes, embed hyperlinks from the start
